data.title: Earnings Snapshot: FedEx beats FQ3 estimates, raises full-year guidance
The market is facing a ‘binary situation’ when it comes to the war in Iran and its consequences
data.description:
Investors face a “binary situation” as Middle East tensions continue to dominate market headlines, according to Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen.

“Either the war escalates, which could create an inflation shock large enough to trigger a recession (and hurt the expensive stock market), or the war eases, and we can focus on what we saw in the first two months of the year, which is growth broadening,” he said.

Nelissen noted that recent comments from U.S. leaders showed “a path to slow de-escalation,” though he cautioned, “These are green shoots at best.”

Mohamed El-Erian, chief economic adviser at Allianz, offered a more cautious assessment of market sentiment.

“Despite the continued escalation in the Middle East War—particularly the latest attacks on energy infrastructure by both Iran and Israel—quite a few market participants are holding on to the view that the global economy is still in a ‘mean reversion’ paradigm,” the strategist said.

He warned that “we are more in the world of ‘multiple equilibrium,’ with each tipping point fueling this dynamic.”

Oil prices (CL1:COM), (CO1:COM) fell after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told media that “the war may end sooner than people think,” according to wire reports. Netanyahu claimed Iran had lost the ability to enrich uranium and make ballistic missiles, adding that Israel was helping the U.S. open the Strait of Hormuz.

U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures (CL1:COM) dropped 2% to around $94 per barrel, while Brent crude (CO1:COM) fell nearly 1% to roughly $106 per barrel. The decline followed earlier spikes after Iran struck a key LNG export facility in Qatar in retaliation for Israel attacking Iran’s South Pars gas field.

Also, President Donald Trump warned that if more facilities in Qatar were attacked, the U.S. would “massively blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field.”

data.title: CD Projekt S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• CD Projekt S.A. press release (OTGLF): In 2025 sales revenues of the CD PROJEKT Group amounted to 867 million PLN, while its consolidated net profit reached 595 million PLN.
  • Diluted earnings per share (in PLN) was 5.90.

data.title: Parsons awarded $47M classified contract extension
data.description:
• Parsons Corporation (PSN) on Thursday said it had been awarded a classified U.S. government contract extension valued at more than $47 million.
  • The award continues work the company has supported for over six years and includes two additional one-year option periods.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Relmada Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.27 misses by $0.13
data.description:
• Relmada Therapeutics press release (RLMD): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.27 misses by $0.13.
  • Research and development expense for the three months ended December 31, 2025, totaled $8.1 million, compared to $11.0 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, a decrease of $2.9 million.
  • General and administrative expense for the three months ended December 31, 2025, totaled $12.3 million compared to $8.1 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, an increase of approximately $4.2 million.

data.title: FedEx Non-GAAP EPS of $5.25 beats by $1.12, revenue of $24B beats by $520M
data.description:
• FedEx press release (FDX): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $5.25 beats by $1.12.
  • Revenue of $24B (+8.1% Y/Y) beats by $520M.

  • Outlook

    FedEx is unable to forecast the fiscal 2026 mark-to-market (“MTM”) retirement plans accounting adjustments. As a result, FedEx is unable to provide a fiscal 2026 earnings per share (“EPS”) or effective tax rate (“ETR”) outlook on a GAAP basis and is relying on the exemption provided by the Securities and Exchange Commission (“SEC”). It is reasonably possible that the fiscal 2026 MTM retirement plans accounting adjustments could have a material effect on fiscal 2026 consolidated financial results and ETR.

    FedEx is revising its fiscal 2026 outlook, and now expects:

    • A 6.0% to 6.5% revenue growth rate year over year, compared to the prior forecast of 5% to 6% growth;
    • Diluted earnings per share of $16.05 to $16.85 before the MTM retirement plans accounting adjustments compared to the prior forecast of $14.80 to $16.00, and $19.30 to $20.10 after also excluding costs related to the planned spin-off of FedEx Freight, business optimization initiatives, the planned change in the company's fiscal year end, and an international regulatory matter, compared to the prior forecast of $17.80 to $19.00;
    • Permanent cost reductions of more than $1 billion in transformation-related savings from structural cost reductions and the advancement of Network 2.0, compared to the prior forecast of $1 billion;
    • An ETR of approximately 24% prior to the MTM retirement plans accounting adjustments, compared to the prior forecast of approximately 25%;
    • Pension contributions of $275 million, compared to the prior forecast of up to $275 million; and
    • Capital spending of no more than $4.1 billion, with a priority on investments in network optimization and efficiency improvement, including fleet and facility modernization and automation, compared to the December forecast of $4.5 billion.
  • Shares +1.65% AH.

data.title: FVCBankcorp extends share buyback program through March 2027
data.description:
• FVCBankcorp (FVCB) on Thursday said its board has extended the company’s share repurchase program through March 31, 2027.
  • The program authorizes the repurchase of up to 1.4 million shares, representing about 8% of outstanding common stock as of December 31, 2025.
  • The company said repurchases may be carried out via open market transactions, block trades or privately negotiated deals, and will be made at management’s discretion depending on market conditions and other factors.
  • FVCBankcorp added that the program may be modified, suspended or terminated at any time and does not obligate the company to repurchase any shares.
  • FVCB +1.23% after hours to $14.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump administration unveils $17B Pennsylvania power plant to be built by NextEra
data.description:
The Trump administration will announce plans for a $17B natural gas power plant to be built in southwestern Pennsylvania, the Pittsburgh Post-Gazette reported Thursday.

The plant, to be called South Mon, is one of three major energy projects stemming from President Trump's $550B trade deal with Japan that are designed to expand power production and support the rapid growth in demand for electricity, according to the report.

The project reportedly will be developed by NextEra Energy (NEE) and generate up to 4.3 GW of power to be connected to existing natural gas pipelines within the Marcellus and Utica shale regions and will be linked to the PJM regional transmission network.

data.title: Signet outlines $6.6B-$6.9B revenue target and brand integration moves amid strategic portfolio shift
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Signet Jewelers Limited (SIG) Q4 2026

Management View

  • CEO James Symancyk opened by stating, "as we announced last week, we delivered at or above the high end of our adjusted operating income and EPS guidance range amidst unprecedented tariffs, record gold costs and a measured consumer while generating 20% more free cash flow on a simplified operating model." He announced that fiscal '27 will focus on accelerating core performance via sharper brand differentiation, broader customer reach, and a more seamless in-store and digital experience.
  • Symancyk emphasized positive comp sales for Kay, Zales, and Jared, noting "Kay, Zales and Jared delivered over 3% combined comp sales growth." He stated that ongoing store renovations have driven incremental low-single-digit comp increases, and that 30% more stores will be renovated this year, touching nearly 10% of the fleet.
  • He stated, "The redesign for each of these brands will provide customers with a more curated selection informed by their behavior with improved navigation," with completion targeted by Q3 for the holiday season.
  • CFO Joan Hilson reported, "We've completed a review of our portfolio and have identified synergies as well as opportunities to integrate stand-alone smaller brands into brands of size to augment core performance and to focus on brands with higher growth potential." She confirmed Blue Nile will be positioned as a premium brand, with James Allen transitioning to a proprietary collection and its website to be sunset in Q2. Rocksbox will become a proprietary collection within Kay.
  • Hilson explained, "Revenue for the quarter was $2.3 billion, with a comp decrease of 0.7%. Excluding James Allen and the net impact of weather, comps grew 1%." She added, "AUR grew 5%, up in all categories."

Outlook

  • Full-year fiscal '27 comp sales are expected to range from down 1.25% to up 2.5%, with total revenue between $6.6 billion and $6.9 billion. Guidance includes $60 million to $80 million of lost sales from the James Allen transition and assumes approximately 100 store closures.
  • Hilson projected, "We expect adjusted operating income between $470 million and $560 million. We expect adjusted EPS between $8.80 and $10.74 per share."
  • Merchandise margin rate for the year is anticipated to be relatively flat at the midpoint of guidance, with pressures in the first half expected to neutralize in the back half.
  • For Q1, comp sales are guided to rise 0.5% to 2.5% with adjusted operating income between $66 million and $77 million.

Financial Results

  • Quarterly revenue was $2.3 billion with a comp decrease of 0.7%. Gross margin was approximately $1 billion, down roughly 60 basis points. Merchandise margin decreased by 30 basis points due to higher commodity costs and tariffs, offset partially by assortment and pricing actions.
  • Adjusted operating income reached $327 million for the quarter. SG&A, excluding incentive comp reset, remained roughly flat; including the reset, SG&A rate increased by about 80 basis points.
  • For fiscal '26, comp sales rose 1.3%, gross margin expanded by 30 basis points, and adjusted operating income grew to $515 million. Adjusted diluted EPS increased by 7%. Free cash flow for the year was approximately $525 million, up 20% from the prior year.
  • Cash at quarter-end totaled $875 million, with total liquidity at roughly $2 billion and an undrawn ABL. Share repurchases totaled $205 million for the year, representing over 7% of shares outstanding.

Q&A

  • Paul Lejuez, Citigroup: Asked about gross margin headwinds for Q1 and learnings from the holiday season. Hilson answered, "the first half definitely has a little bit more pressure, and we believe the back half begins to neutralize in terms of the GMM impact related to tariffs and commodities." Symancyk said, "business overall continued to strengthen as we came through the quarter... That actually worked well for us at Christmas, and I think was part of what really led to building momentum through the quarter."
  • Lorraine Maikis, BofA Securities: Inquired about the lab-grown diamond (LGD) business. Symancyk said, "Lab-grown diamond fashion, in particular, continues to grow at a higher rate...the opportunity for growth there, we continue to see as outsized relative to the rest of diamond jewelry." Penetration for lab-grown fashion during the holiday grew to just over 20%.
  • Randal Konik, Jefferies: Asked about liquidity and share repurchases. Hilson said, "We had a $2 billion liquidity at the end of the year. It's $500 million over our target... There's nothing in our way to continue to exercise that capital." Year-to-date, $45 million in shares were repurchased through March 17.
  • Irwin Boruchow, Wells Fargo: Asked about merchandise margins and comp revenue spreads due to digital brand repositioning. Hilson clarified the impact of James Allen’s transition and noted, "the impact of James Allen was roughly 1 point in Q4, a little over 1 point."
  • Jonathan Keypour, Goldman Sachs: Queried about supply chain impacts from UAE and Zales brand performance. Symancyk said, "we have built a much more flexible supply chain in -- really in advance of all of the mitigation efforts around tariffs... we haven't seen any disruption as it relates to goods."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ questions focused on margin pressures, portfolio transitions, liquidity deployment, and the performance of key brands, indicating a neutral to slightly positive tone with some pressing for clarification on headwinds and mitigation strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing operational improvements, cost controls, and the positive start to fiscal '27. Direct statements such as "We don't see anything in our way" and "we are, we believe, positioned to start the year for good selling within our assortments" reflect this.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's prepared remarks and responses showed increased confidence tied to their ability to deliver and manage through volatility, while analysts remained attentive to risk factors but less cautious than in Q3.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from a cautious approach in Q3, which highlighted consumer softness and tariff volatility, to a more confident outlook for fiscal '27, with specific revenue and EPS targets.
  • Strategic focus evolved from broad efforts to mitigate tariffs and manage assortment gaps in Q3 to targeted brand integration, store renovations, and digital strategy in Q4.
  • Analysts in the current quarter emphasized follow-up on brand transitions, margin impacts, and capital allocation, whereas Q3 questions centered more on holiday readiness and tariff mitigation.
  • Key metric changes include the transition of James Allen and the repositioning of Blue Nile, as well as a shift in reported comps and revenue guidance to reflect these structural changes.
  • Management’s confidence increased, supported by operational improvements, while analysts’ tone shifted from caution to seeking clarity on transition impacts and future growth levers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted continued exposure to commodity volatility and tariffs but stated, "we have a longer lead time to address these headwinds with the following actions: select pricing actions related to commodities, reduced off-holiday discounting, increased LGD mix, assortment architecture, and to a lesser degree, benefits from gold hedges."
  • Guidance reflects lost sales from James Allen’s transition and store closures, with revenue expected to be impacted by $60 million to $80 million.
  • Analysts questioned potential disruptions from supply chain or geopolitical factors, but management reported no material impacts and described the supply chain as flexible and resilient.

Final Takeaway

Signet management emphasized delivering at the high end of guidance during a challenging year, with strategic portfolio consolidation, accelerated store renovations, and enhanced digital experiences planned to drive growth in fiscal '27. Brand rationalization, targeted capital deployment, and a balanced approach to commodity and tariff headwinds are expected to support continued free cash flow generation and operating margin expansion. The transition of digital brands and focus on core engines—along with proactive cost management—position the company to capitalize on positive sales momentum as it advances its Grow Brand Love strategy into its second year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: CTT - Correios De Portugal, S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• CTT - Correios De Portugal, S.A. press release (CTTPY): FY Net profit3 of €50.7M (+€5.2M; +11.4% vs. FY24).
  • Revenue of €1.29B (+16.2% Y/Y).
